evidence_label: case-001 curative affordability gap matrix, not medical advice

source_checks.source_role: Allogeneic HCT is the curative transplant benchmark and requires donor-route review, pre-transplant workup, myeloablative conditioning, expert-center care, and follow-up.
Autologous gene addition and gene editing require HSPC collection, product manipulation, myeloablation, infusion, monitoring, and long-term follow-up.
Regulator anchor for exagamglogene autotemcel as a gene-edited cell therapy category for TDT patients 12 years and older.
Regulator anchor for betibeglogene autotemcel as a gene-addition product category for beta-thalassemia requiring regular RBC transfusions.
Managed-access appraisal anchor for remaining uncertainty around durability, complications, withdrawal before infusion, standard-care modeling, and cost effectiveness.
Keeps HSCT, autologous gene-cell therapy, non-curative drugs, stabilization, and registry benchmarks separate before specialist discussion.

route_gap_matrix.research_use: Use as the affordability and durability benchmark. Do not infer case suitability.

route_gap_matrix.benchmark_role: curative autologous gene-addition or gene-editing benchmark
route_gap_matrix.research_use: Use as the closest biology benchmark. Do not infer access, screening, or eligibility.
Use only to benchmark burden reduction, not cure.

route_gap_matrix.benchmark_role: required safety baseline before advanced-route review
route_gap_matrix.research_use: Use to identify missing records and stabilization blockers.
Use as public benchmark context only until a specialist asks a specific screening question.

decision_rule: The affordable-cure research lane may prioritize explicit benchmark gaps, but it cannot recommend therapy, imply case eligibility, imply access, or bypass qualified specialist review.
